output.hypothesis: For a fixed single-GPU launch configuration, a 2048 KB working set with 128-byte strided global-memory accesses will produce lower effective bandwidth than a cache-friendly regime, indicating sensitivity to memory-hierarchy locality under otherwise identical controls.
output.rationale: The research question directly matches the bounded CUDA memory-hierarchy probe family. This configuration stays within the single-GPU scope, uses fixed launch parameters and seed for determinism, and is aggressive enough on working-set size and stride to make the locality hypothesis falsifiable within the round budget.
output.selected_question: explore memory hierarchy effects with strided global memory access
